items.title: ForestDirectory Decks/Browse strip → nested TabWidget
items.description: ForestDirectory.vue implements its Decks/Browse sub-tab strip with plain buttons + a local ref rather than the reusable TabWidget every other tab strip uses. Functional parity; refactor unifies the pattern (loses a little literal-union precision). Trigger: alongside other ForestDirectory work, or a focused single-file session.

items.title: Internationalization (i18n) — string sweep
items.description: vue-i18n plumbing + the 150-300 hardcoded-string sweep landed (schema 23→24, LocalePicker, en source + zh-CN/ja/ko LLM-drafted stubs). Residual: native-speaker review per locale before each catalog is marked Active. A future structured-backend-error-codes arc is its own dispatch.

items.title: Chess clone (domain port)
items.description: Per ADR-0003 portability: implement PositionNormalizerPort for PGN (backend), replace the ~30-40% Band-3 frontend surface (SGF/board/KataGo wire), add a Stockfish/Leela engine bridge, substitute the palette stdlib. Trigger: a chess-playing contributor with a PoC and willingness to do the engine bridge.

items.title: Silent-coercion-at-protocol-boundaries audit
items.description: Sweep for closed-set wire vocabularies parsed with an open-set default that silently coerces unknown values (the ADR-0002 silent-coercion shape; bitten more than once). Cure: enum/Literal witness, one source of truth for the wire-string↔enum map, distinguish missing-key from unknown-value, two-tier loudness. Frontend + backend; proxy via dispatch.

items.title: Responsive design — deferred items
items.description: Items deferred from the 23-iter responsive arc: user-discretionary (10px body font ignoring browser prefs, details-open defaults, board scroll-hijack, SidebarWidget v-show) and narrow-viewport bug-class (registry-editor right-pane wrap, tree-panel overflow, KnobSlider label truncation, timeline clip, log auto-reveal). None user-blocking at 4K. Trigger: user-prioritised, interleaved on feat/responsive.

items.title: Save → disconnect clears the analysis graph; companion always-persist option
items.description: Clicking Save then disconnecting clears the in-memory analysis graph (staleness-avoidance default, sound but UX-surprising). Two-part fix: keep the default; add an [experimental] profile.settings.engine.katago.alwaysPersistGraphs toggle with a strong resource-cost warning (~1 MB per game-worth). Trigger: user-prioritised.

items.title: Byte-XOR delta on the Q8 ownership wire
items.description: Registry value v2-quantized-hifi-xor + a byte-stable encoder scheme tag; makeLossyEncoder absorbs a byteXorDelta param. Reconstruction byte-identical to plain hifi (XOR is algebraic); no backend changes. The sole Pareto-improving operating point from the compression saturation arc.

items.title: Navigation re-render localization — App-decouple (perf Arc 2)
items.description: App.vue re-rendered the whole tree on every nav step (reading the move cursor in its template). Arc 2 moved cursor reads into leaf components (StatusBar self-sources turn/captures/moveNumber; TreeWidget self-sources currentNodeId) so App stops re-rendering on navigation, eliminating the per-step whole-tree patch.

items.title: Navigation-during-range-query perf (regime B)
items.description: During a live range query the median frame ran ~47ms (~3.3x the 60fps budget), substantiated 2026-05-29. Umbrella over three levers (RB-1/RB-2/RB-3, tracked as children). RB-1/RB-2 shipped; RB-3 dropped. Residual: whether the regime-B frame cost is fully retired wants a re-profile (ADR-0009). Trigger: user-prioritised.

items.title: RB-1 — App ↔ engine-metrics decouple
items.description: App re-rendered the whole tree on every metric tick (531x); Toolbar now self-sources status/metrics → 0. Cleared the metric-driven whole-tree re-render (~1s). Modest frame impact alone.

items.title: RB-2 — analysis-panel chart-update coalescing
items.description: The dominant ~4.5s cost (ECharts setOption per packet + AnalysisChartPanel thrash). Resolved by the analysis-panel refactor (render active tab only over the panel registry) plus PR #329's collapsed-chart active-gate; inactive/collapsed panels no longer run setOption. Re-profile pending before the win is claimed.

items.title: RB-3 — packet-receive-path chunking
items.description: Hypothesised chunking lever. Re-measured on current main: onAnalysisUpdate is ~99ms total (p50 0.2ms/packet), NOT the ~2.35s the pre-refactor audit attributed to it. No bottleneck, no lever — dropped. The named Effect-TS reserve trigger did not fire.

items.title: Perceptual event projection — user-observable event-stream chart
items.description: From a captured perf trace, reconstruct the event stream as the user observes it (input→visual-response latency, stalls). The load-bearing idea is an ACL: a perceptual-event vocabulary independent of the SPA's markers, with a trace→vocabulary adapter. Canvas swimlane timeline (ADR-0010). Depends on the faithful headed-capture path.

items.title: Memory-profiling discipline — fold onto the perf-capture harness
items.description: scripts/perf-heap.mjs (CDP HeapProfiler driver; retained-heap tail-slope metric added to ADR-0009). Board/analysis lifecycle paths found leak-free; trace memory counters surfaced without a flag; resetWorkspace-churn scenario clean. Both follow-on sessions landed 2026-06-01.

items.title: Unified user-controllable-scalar surface (knob registry)
items.description: Knob-registry substrate (types + path-walk accessors + named-transform library + ownership state machine), the cross-domain KnobRegistryEditor, the qEUBO consumer migration, and the first magic-literals promotion batch. Open follow-ups tracked as separate items.

items.title: qEUBO bookmark schema reshape
items.description: Reshape qEUBO bookmarks from Record<string,number> to Record<KnobId,number[]>. Deferred follow-up from the knob-registry arc.

items.title: Wire-key derivation from KnobDecl ids for qEUBO controlled_parameters
items.description: Derive qEUBO's controlled_parameters wire keys from KnobDecl ids. Backend coordination via dispatch if pursued. Deferred follow-up from the knob-registry arc.

items.title: docs/pre-merge-checklist.md as an actual file
items.description: A template the trusted rotation consults when filing retroactive correctives (NOT a merge-blocking gate — that framing was the wrong enforcement point). Surfaced by the toolbar-popover postmortem; one focused PR closes it.

items.title: Classification-discipline principle as its own tenet; Rule 7 relocation
items.description: Discharged by ADR-0008 (Classification Discipline, Accepted 2026-05-17) — the standalone-tenet articulation this item asked for. The literal 'relocate ADR-0002 Rule 7' was deliberately declined: Rule 7 stays in ADR-0002 as the fail-loudly-register instance with its provisional-home flag retired, while ADR-0008 hosts the broader principle. The tenet got its own home, so superseded rather than shipped.

items.title: Single CTE per pipeline run
items.description: PipelineExecutor.run makes one fetch_selection call covering all context_ids (one CTE keyed off card_source.card_id IN (:context_ids), first-seen-by-MIN(depth) dedup in Python). Shipped silently inside the item-32a port-purification arc, which predates the PR-tracked history; 2026-04-26 is the squash-floor 'present-by' date (helpers exist comment-tagged 'Item 30c' in the initial commit), not a proven landing date. The old 'do 30d first' dependency is moot (both present in the same tree).

items.title: Consolidate the four recursive-CTE implementations
items.description: Shipped as the single _recursive_descent_cte helper (renamed from the proposed _build_lineage_cte) that the live sites delegate to (fetch_lineage, _build_selection_cte, fetch_tree_by_root) — the 'one recursive skeleton, bug-fixes propagate' goal is met. Landed inside the item-32a port-purification arc (which relocated two of the originally-cited sites); the literal four-site framing was overtaken by 32a. 2026-04-26 is the squash-floor 'present-by' date. The two un-folded duplicates (tree_queries.py, tree_dsl.py) are dead/test-only, tracked separately as remove-dead-recursive-cte-duplicates.

items.title: Item 27 full — ETag-based multi-tab coordination
items.description: Multi-tab use isn't a known workflow; SyncService is last-write-wins (documented, item 27-min shipped). If multi-tab becomes real, an ETag-based coordination layer is the right design (sketch in the SyncService::sendSync() comment).

items.title: Item 32 — zeroconf / mDNS service discovery
items.description: Backend zeroconf advertisement + frontend discovery, replacing the fixed-URL config. Large because the test matrix is wide (3 OSes x browsers x network configs) and failure modes need graceful fallback to the configured URL. Constraints: no mandatory Linux deps (no Avahi), Windows OOTB, Firefox without extensions.

items.title: Tag-DSL virtual-tag macro language
items.description: Shipped across PRs #197 (file split), #198 (macro language + three caps), #199 (reference + REPL). Negation-in-definitions and parenthesised grouping now supported. Closed the long-carrying tag_dsl.py-is-an-adapter rough edge.

items.title: Automatic mistake discovery
items.description: Surface engine-defined mistake candidates from a played-out game as a review queue. Open design questions: mistake definition (delta-from-best / cluster / palette-signal), threshold scalar(s) (KnobDecl candidates), affordance (live / batch / both), heredity (spawn child cards or standalone). MergedDeltaPanel peaks are a useful candidate source. Needs the design decisions settled.

items.title: Inline analysis_config editing in the card-metadata panel
items.description: The arc-2 inline-edit panel treats analysis_config as read-only (deferred). Backend reads only gamma; the rest is opaque pass-through, so the frontend owns the shape. Today users edit it via the registry editor's full-shape JSON UI; question is whether an inline surface adds enough common-case affordance. Trigger: user thought-time.

items.title: Polymorphic chart renderer abstraction
items.description: A ChartRenderer Port-equivalent (ADR-0003) so the chart surface can host alternative backends (custom canvas, D3+canvas, Plotly), motivated by ECharts' destroy-and-recreate-every-Rect heatmap redraw. Open questions: seam location, Port surface, theme coupling, bundle weight. Not blocking; the heatmap throttle bought headroom to defer.

items.title: Analysis tab — user-customisable sub-tabs binding chart components
items.description: Panel registry + multi-tab (render only the active tab's panels) + a Settings tab-layout editor; migration 54→55 backfills default named tabs. Subsumes the collapsed-chart per-packet setOption concern that docs/notes/deferred-items.md separately tracked. (The worked Lapse-2 exhibit in the 2026-06-01 RCA: documented open for two days after shipping.)

items.title: Content-addressed card identity — auditability investigation
items.description: Investigation, not implementation: would making content_hash the primary card identity (game_source joins as incidental provenance) improve auditability? Open questions: what 'canonical position' means, auditability of what specifically, schema-migration vs interface-code cost. Implementation follows only if the investigation answers yes.

items.title: Doc-graph SVG — Graphviz dot spline-routing layout failure
items.description: dot -Tsvg on the ~330-node graph exits non-zero (routesplines: cannot find NORMAL edge) while still emitting a complete SVG — a real layout failure for ≥1 edge, not just a warning. Non-critical (manifest is SoT). Stopgap tolerates the non-zero exit. Version-sensitive (CI's apt dot, not local 14.1.2).

items.title: Doc-graph SVG — render off the counted tree
items.description: The committed SVG was removed and gitignored (GitHub counts every line; .gitattributes -diff doesn't decount). Interim honest step. The real fix: a CI job renders from the committed manifest to a dedicated render branch or Pages, with deterministic edge sorting so structural changes are minimal JSON diffs.

items.title: Analysis-panel container-query recompute under chart redraw (de-CQ the preview-hide)
items.description: Replaced the @container query with a ResizeObserver-toggled .narrow class in AnalysisChartPanel.vue (fires on real width changes, not per style flush). Headless before/after: forced style+layout invocations −19.4%, no regression. Modest (~2% of wall) but clean.

items.title: Adaptive-query cancellation leak (mid-adaptive terminate — likely proxy-side)
items.description: Cancelling an adaptive range query mid-adaptive leaves the proxy query running while the SPA drops further packets. The SPA's one-id/one-terminate model is correct per the unified-id contract; leading hypothesis is proxy-side (terminate not routed to the running adaptive sub-query). Not confirmed — needs proxy runtime visibility before drawing conclusions. If proxy-side, file a dispatch + coordinated bump.

items.title: --surface-1 backgrounds (low-contrast on the default theme)
items.description: var(--surface-1) resolves dark-grey under black default text — almost never the right background (--surface-0 is the default). The Phase-3 editor was fixed; a few other call sites remain unaudited. grep + excise non-deliberate usages or annotate the genuine exceptions. Low priority.

items.title: Scattered non-coalescing timing literals
items.description: timing.ts centralises coalescing windows; the adjacent timeouts/durations/interaction-delays (KATAGO_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_MS, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS, REVEAL_DURATION_MS, DEFAULT_CLOSE_DELAY_MS) remain scattered — each magic-literal compliant but not catalogued. Decide: leave / sibling catalog / sectioned timing.ts. Low priority.

items.title: ADR-effectiveness audits + doc-graph consolidation discipline
items.description: The ADRs are policy, not mechanism; a periodic per-tenet audit (where does this tenet not hold, and why) would surface drift. ADR-0002 most overdue. The maintainer settled the doc-consolidation leg 2026-06-01 (the work-status SSOT, RCA G5 — being built now); the broader mandated reorganization discipline (doc retirement, taxonomy, docs/notes hierarchy) and the per-tenet audit cadence remain open.

items.title: Serial numbers on compiler-generated artifacts
items.description: Generated files (notably frontend/src/types/backend.ts) carry no in-file marker of which backend revision they correspond to. A short serial (timestamp / content hash / git SHA) in a header comment would let downstream readers verify the version. Draft an RFC (format, generation hook, embedding location, consumer validation) before any implementation.

items.title: LoadAction type is dishonest (ConfirmLoadModal.vue)
items.description: ConfirmLoadModal now exposes Promise<LoadResult> with the structured { action, remember } pair; the as-LoadAction cast is gone.

items.title: Silent guard fail in handleLoadCardFromDatabase (App.vue)
items.description: useDirtyBoardGuard owns the policy; the silent early-return is replaced with an explicit throw if the modal ref is null. The old App.vue handler no longer exists; the contract is in the composable's JSDoc.

items.title: Engine connection lifecycle on logout (deployment-model-dependent)
items.description: resetWorkspace intentionally does NOT reset store.engine: today the WebSocket URL is not user-keyed, so the same socket serves both users honestly; half-resetting would be an ADR-0001 lie. Revisit when the deployment model makes the WS URL user-keyed (cloud KataGo, rented per-user analysis, auth-bearing tokens) — then reset engine + wire analysisService.disconnect() into the reset.

items.title: Remove legacy auth-key compat shim (api-client.ts)
items.description: api-client.ts carries a one-shot migrateLegacyAuthKeys() shim (ebisu_* → auth_* on module init). Per ADR-0002 exception #3, a bounded-and-scheduled-for-removal shim. Remove the function + its single call (~30 lines) once monitoring confirms no users carry the legacy keys.

items.title: Tags-fetch hydration race (useAppBootstrap.ts)
items.description: useAppBootstrap fires getTags() concurrently with sync.connect()'s hydration; if getTags wins, hydration's updateFromRemote overwrites the profile and drops knownTags. Pre-existing, benign (knownTags is re-fetchable), but a real ordering bug. Fix: await hydration before the tags fetch, or move knownTags to a watcher that re-applies on identity change.

items.title: Refactoring queue from ADR-0007
items.description: The C2 arc (2026-04-27) shipped the ADR-0007 file-size refactor for App.vue (593→500 via three composable extractions: useResizablePanel, useDirtyBoardGuard, useAppBootstrap), and concluded the strict ≤250 SFC target is especially hard in .vue files (bulk is template+styles, not logic). Operative policy since: incremental-per-ADR-0004 + CSS-contraction (ADR-0007 §Format single-line styling) + bounded-stopping, never compress logic. This item now tracks only the residual backlog — files still over budget, most GROWN since (illustrative snapshot, not per-file status: App.vue ~708, PaletteEditor ~626, useReviewSession ~664, BaseChart ~597, ForestDirectory ~500, HorizontalTimelineVisualizer ~513 at components/tree/, MintCardModal ~396; types.ts ~2245 keeps the type-catalogue exception). Handled on touch; no batch, no named next-targets (the priority-queue framing was retired by the bounded-stopping posture).

items.title: Review-state convention inconsistency between App.vue and BoardTab.vue
items.description: BoardTab.vue .review-complete uses --state-success (green); App.vue's review-state ternary uses --accent-secondary (orange) on FINISHED, --state-attention (red) otherwise. Either legitimately different anchors needing clearer naming, or off-convention. Decide what each renders, then adopt the --review-* aliases on App.vue or introduce a separate anchor.

items.title: PV-overlay typography proportions — calibration question
items.description: MoveSuggestions.vue carries five stoneR-relative multipliers (1.01 outline; 0.72/0.58/0.82 font-size hierarchy; 0.62 score-label offset) likely co-calibrated by eye for the PV-preview rhythm. Naming them individually without naming the calibration loses context. The 60ms fade was promoted to a knob (2026-05-22); the five multipliers remain deferred.

items.title: PV-animation defaults — pairwise-calibration question
items.description: use-pv-animation.ts declares its own stepDelayMs/windowDurationMs/fadeDurationMs/pvOpacity defaults, duplicating defaults.ts. They may be pairwise-calibrated for the repeating-window rhythm (co-tuned constants — a third pattern beyond snap-by-cluster and decouple-via-alias). Determine whether coupled; if so name the calibration, else consolidate-via-import.

items.title: Mistake-finder un-punished-flag brittleness
items.description: The un-punished red-flag heuristic (per-board worst-15% quantile) false-positives on clean games (marginal move + marginal opponent move). Detecting mistakes from a single net's terminal eval is the brittle special case, not a fixable heuristic bug. Avenues: absolute-severity floor, cross-net agreement via SELECTOR, within-position stability, cross-palette agreement.

items.title: Stability surface — distribution-level (information-geometric) metric
items.description: The Stage-4 stability substrate uses categorical extractors; the maintainer's intuition wants a distribution-level metric over the full visit distribution (information length / cumulative Bayesian surprise / pairwise JS). Different abstraction class (packet→ProbabilityVector, no changepoint compression). Reasonable as a parallel substrate alongside the v1 categorical one.

items.title: KDE boundary bias for bounded-support palettes
items.description: The Stage-3 Gaussian KDE leaks mass past bounded supports ([0,1] quality/rank palettes show nonzero density at x<0). Functional impact cosmetic today. Disciplined fixes: reflection method (cheapest), boundary kernels, transformation (logit), beta-kernel KDE. Substrate: a per-palette support?:[number,number] field threaded to the KDE consumer.

items.title: Stringly-encoded API errors reverse-engineered downstream (audit + RCA)
items.description: api-client threw a stringly-typed API Error <status>: <body>; six consumers reparsed it by regex — a brittleness hazard (silent degrade on format drift, no compile error). Emergency fix (PR #318) introduced class ApiError { status, body } and converted all six. The exhaustiveness audit closed 2026-06-01 (the six ARE the complete population; 0 new sites), and the RCA's pending-review residual resolved on merge to origin/main.

items.title: Semantic-clarity refactors surfaced by the effect-typing consult arc
items.description: Two liked-in-principle-but-undecided refactors: (a) name the analysis-service packet multiplexer (onAnalysisUpdate fans one packet to ~5 disjoint sinks — a Subject; caveat: sinks are heterogeneous, so named-dispatch + dependency-inversion may beat a generic Subject); (b) unify the three multi-variant error encodings onto the discriminated-union + never-default idiom (overlaps/subsumed by the ApiError work).

items.title: Explicit documentation-graph artifact with commit-age heatmap
items.description: A git-driven generator (zero-dep Node, shells to dot) emitting a machine-readable manifest + a rendered graph + a staleness heatmap + a broken-reference report; committed and CI-verified-fresh. 330 nodes / 1663 edges at ship. Manifest-first: the doc-link validator falls out of the same pass.

items.title: ForestStat / TagStat — wire-shape passthrough at the ACL boundary
items.description: mapForestStat translator added; ForestStat rewritten to camelCase with branded ids, nullable metadata preserved per ADR-0002. mapTagStat added as a structural-redundancy stub (field-identical) with a doc comment naming the redundancy — settled direction for future accidental-passthrough ACLs.

items.title: Anchor role overloading in the chrome substrate
items.description: Decouple-via-alias landed: five role aliases (--player-black/-white, --review-active/-intermission/-complete) added to theme.css and ChromeAnchor; chart and review-state sites swept to use them. Visual unchanged; future tuning can break the aliasing without disturbing chrome. Settled direction recorded in the theming plan.

items.title: Distribution packaging
items.description: Make the software installable for users without Node/Python toolchains — the leading edge of the post-v1 arc and the structural blocker between 'tenancy spine works' and 'hosted deployment ships'. Options memo on disk.

items.title: Public deployment (hosted service)
items.description: Once tenancy is real and distribution packaging lands, move from local-install to hosted service. The proxy's RELAY-over-LEAF pattern makes the engine side scalable.

items.title: qEUBO palette calibration — end-to-end validation
items.description: qEUBO is feature-complete (backend wrapper, REST routes, useQeubo, toolbar A/B, parameter-meta editor, bookmarks UI). End-to-end UI smoke with Redis is the remaining validation gate; runtime is opt-in (QEUBO_ENABLED=False). On validation, transition the qEUBO design note to implemented.

items.title: Community palette library
items.description: Palettes are JSON; a share-my-palette upload/download flow, hosted alongside the backend or as a static-site feature. Depends on whether palette calibration produces results the community wants to share. Longer-term horizon.

items.title: gradingParameter opacity — tighten if the inner shape stabilizes
items.description: gradingParameter is Record<string,any> because the inner shape is application-defined and changes; the SR composable reads gradingParameter.data.analysis_config per card. If the inner shape stabilizes enough to deserve a typed schema, formalize it in types.ts and tighten the access sites rather than letting Record<string,any> become permanent through inertia.

items.title: Remove the dead recursive-CTE duplicates + retire the D-5/D-6 dead-code tests
items.description: Residual the 30d/32a consolidation left behind. Delete domain/tree_queries.py::get_lineage_cte (zero importers anywhere) and domain/tree_dsl.py::SubtreeSelection.to_cte (imported only by the D-5/D-6 tests in test_cte_lineage.py, which document bugs in code production cannot reach — the live SubtreeSelection/ContextSelection are the unrelated pipeline_dsl.py classes). Retire D-5/D-6; keep CTE-1…8, which exercise the live LineageRepository.fetch_lineage path. Closes the dead-variant hazard that undercuts 30d's 'one place' goal.

items.title: Close / minimize / restore open boards (tab-management UX)
items.description: A way to close, minimize, and restore open games — tab-manager browser extensions are possible inspiration. Related rough edge: closing a board renames the others (reasonable but confusing). From the frontend backlog.

items.title: Policy-head outputs overlay
items.description: Render KataGo policy-head outputs as a board overlay, reusing the parameterised BoardHeatmapOverlay.vue + decodeBoardArray (frontend-side linear→2D mapping; strip the trailing 'pass' slot before decode), so no proxy middleware extension is needed. The open residual of the shipped ownership-maps work.

items.title: Mouse-scrollable PV stepping
items.description: PVs should be steppable manually via the scroll-wheel — a manual analog of the existing animated PV display. From the frontend backlog.

items.title: Offload styling/layout to ecosystem libraries
items.description: Direction (unspecified): offload some styling/layout to existing JS/TS/Vue libraries (grid-layout, Monaco/CodeMirror, etc.) rather than hand-rolling. Composes with the responsive-design work. From the frontend backlog.

items.title: Card metadata display during active review sessions
items.description: The SR session shows status/counter/scores but not the card's tags, parent lineage, or notes — useful when reviewing a card pulled from a months-old game. The half of the visits-override bullet that didn't ship.

items.title: Card editor
items.description: A dedicated card editor; likely shares DRY/reuse with card-metadata-during-review and the inline-edit panel. From the frontend backlog.

items.title: Many open boards slow the app down
items.description: With many boards open the whole application gets slower, noticed when fast-navigating a game (scroll-wheel or held arrow keys). A scaling/perf bug, distinct from the regime-B range-query cost.

items.title: PV-hover jank during a live range query
items.description: Hovering a PV is janky while a range query is running; cause unclear. Treated as a bug; needs investigation (missed during the last perf session). Adjacent to the regime-B perf cluster but a distinct hover-path symptom.

items.title: Tag / favorite games in the library
items.description: Let users tag and favorite games in the library. The forward-compatible shape is unsettled (shape TBD). Touches game_source (backend) + the library UI.

items.title: Configuration schema + equivariant projections
items.description: Introduce one declarative configuration schema S as the SSOT for each config path's structure, presentation, grouping, and gating, and derive every config UI as a pure projection of S — the recursive registry editor, the generated Settings sub-tabs/rolldowns, the knob editor, and the toolbar quick-access popover. Equivariance: every projection of a leaf reduces to one canonical write(path, v) (Law 1), and a schema change propagates to all projections with no per-projection bookkeeping (Law 2). Composes with (does not subsume) the knob registry via a knob-ref widget. Schema-first: phases 0-3 are additive metadata describing the existing tree (no data migration) — P0 schema types + instance + a bijection test, P1 registry editor reads S, P2 sections generated from declared placement, P3 one schema-validated mutator + knob delegation; P4 (deferred) = end-user/LLM authorability via a visual designer or a card-set-style schema DSL. The 'hierarchical options menu' the toolbar-sliders backlog bullet asked for falls out as one projection. design-note: planned.

items.title: Tag-DSL-filterable browse directory (+ fetch-all deck-DSL)
items.description: Make the browse directory tag-DSL filterable; and investigate a tree/deck-DSL form that fetches all cards, so the browse fetcher could be a deck fetcher rather than a distinct endpoint — consolidating duplicated code and reducing drift. Shape unsettled. Touches the DSL (backend) + browse UI.

items.title: Bulk card management over sub-trees
items.description: Bulk operations over card sub-corpuses: suspend/unsuspend a subtree (or by game provenance), bulk tagging, and changing visits across a subtree (e.g. when better-but-costlier weights ship) — an fmap-over-subtree shape. May warrant baking 'suspended' into the tree-DSL and refactoring the DSL. Shape unsettled.

items.title: Rename a tag
items.description: Let users rename an existing tag. From the local frontend backlog.

items.title: CI advisory: retire shipped design notes into the archive
items.description: Design notes are proliferating (load-bearing, must-read docs that accumulate). The SSOT links shipped items to their design-note refs, so a check can flag live design notes (in docs/notes/, not docs/archive/) whose only referencing SSOT items are closed → archival candidates. Advisory, not a hard gate (archival is an editorial judgment; a note may retain residual value). Archival breaks cross-references, so it costs a cross-reference audit to reach homeostasis — the consolidation-xref-fallout.md pattern is the template. Sequenced for immediately after the surrender-status step. Part of the adr-effectiveness-audits doc-retirement leg.

items.title: Add a GitHub-style labels facet to the work-status schema
items.description: Add an optional, multi-valued `labels` facet (GitHub-style tags) to work-status items for free specificity — e.g. bug, feature-request, performance, architectural-cruft, refactor, investigation. Decided 2026-06-02 AGAINST a deep closed kind-taxonomy (issue|feature|refactor|…): labels are long-lived and battle-tested, and a deep taxonomy invites the ADR-0008 forced-classification trap. The issue⊃bug relation (an issue need not be a bug; a bug is always an issue) and cross-cutting concerns like performance are expressed via labels, not a kind hierarchy. Labels drawn from a closed-but-amendable allowed set (type-sane; prevents perf/performance drift; reversible to free-form if set-maintenance grates). Backfill across the corpus is incremental. Sequenced after the surrender-status step.

items.title: Co-change advisory for derived docs (source-changed-without-derived)
items.description: A derived doc declares its sources inline (`<!-- derived-from: <glob> -->`); on a PR, tools/doc-graph/cochange-advisory.mjs flags any derived doc whose source changed but which was not itself updated — closing the gap the stale ADR synopsis exposed (the doc-graph resolves edges + tracks age, but not derivation-direction content staleness; ADR-0005 Rule 3 hazard). Per-PR-diff (origin/main...HEAD), not state-based, so it cannot nag forever; silence a genuine non-update with `cochange-ack: <derived-doc> — <reason>` in a commit (per-PR, self-documenting). Advisory only (exit 0). Initial curated declarations: adr-synopsis.md ⟵ docs/adr/*.md, TODO.md ⟵ work-status.json.

items.title: SyncService suspend/resume affordance (capture-neutral persistence)
items.description: The perf-capture harness — and prospectively the headless-capture, perceptual-event-projection, and autonomous-SRS drivers — drive the SPA programmatically but must not mutate the user's persisted profile. Today this is handled per-setting and fragile: scenarioContext passes the proxy URL transiently to analysisService.connect() and snapshots/restores the adaptive toggle in teardown (the PR #348 stop-gap), and separately closes scenario-created boards — each a hand-rolled neutrality patch, and every new setting a capture touches needs its own. The robust solution is a first-class affordance to SUSPEND and RESUME SyncService persistence around an ephemeral run, so the whole run is capture-neutral by construction (subsuming the board-cleanup + the URL/adaptive restore). Design forks to weigh at pickup: the --connect-to-a-live-session case — suspending persistence in the maintainer's real session must NOT silently drop genuine concurrent edits, so the suspend scopes to harness-owned contexts; the precise primitive (suspend/resume vs. an ephemeral scratch-profile overlay vs. a dry-run flag); and app-wide fallout. Deliberately deferred 2026-06-03 — it needs fallout analysis and composes with the headless / projection / autonomous-SRS arcs, better done as one considered refactor than piecemeal. PR #348's per-setting handling is the interim stop-gap.
